---
title: "RP 11: Emotional Buildup"
format: html
---
# RP 11: Emotional Buildup

Emotional buildup happens when stress, resentment, fear, or sadness accumulates without release. People often do not notice buildup until urges or conflict spike. By the time emotions feel overwhelming, choices narrow and relapse risk rises.

Preventing buildup means using regular emotional check-ins and early regulation skills. Small daily releases (talking, writing, movement, breathing, problem-solving) are more effective than waiting for a crisis.

Emotional buildup often develops when stress is repeatedly postponed rather than processed. Daily emotional release strategies reduce pressure accumulation and lower the chance of impulsive coping through alcohol.

## Daily Emotional Pressure Gauge
| Day | My pressure level (0-10) | What is driving it? | One release action I took |
|---|---:|---|---|
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |

::: {.callout-tip}
### Try this this week

Use the steps below as a practical experiment and notice what shifts.

If pressure reaches 7 or higher, use two release actions within the same day.

Briefly record what worked best so you can keep the strongest strategy.
:::
